chunks.text: Guys, in today's video we are going to see function caching.  Function caching is used when one function is run for one value.  In this case, we use the memoization technique.  Let's go to the computer screen and see how all these things work.  Let's get started.

chunks.title: Function Caching in Python
chunks.text: Intro  So, first of all, understand what is the concept of function caching.  Let's say I have a very expensive function.  Expensive function means computationally expensive function.  Which is taking 10 seconds to run.

chunks.title: Function Caching in Python
chunks.text: 10 seconds is a lot in computational terms.  Now, let's say it is doing some computations for value 2.  It is taking data from the network and it is running in 10 seconds.  Now it is running for value 3 in 11 seconds.  It is running for value 4 in 9 seconds.

chunks.title: Function Caching in Python
chunks.text: Now let's say that the domain for which you are going to run the function.  You must know the concept of domain and range.  If you don't know, then it's okay.  Let's say that the function for which we are going to run is limited.  Let's say I know that my function is mostly run for single digit numbers.

chunks.title: Function Caching in Python
chunks.text: So, if I am running it 1000 times, then what will be the average time of running it?  1000 x 10 means 10000 seconds.  But I can reduce this time by using function caching.  What happens? How does it work?  What happens is that your program maintains a cache.

chunks.title: Function Caching in Python
chunks.text: Cache maintains means that if once the function is run for value 2 and the result is 36.  Then it will remember this and store it.  That for value 2, the value of this function is 36.  Next time there will be computation for value 2.  So don't calculate.
